title: Vasuda Sinha
content:
Partner | Co-Lead, International Arbitration Group
People.ContactEmail vsinha@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
With extensive experience in international commercial arbitration and investor-state arbitration, Vasuda’s global experience helps turn clients’ cross-jurisdictional challenges into opportunities.
Vasuda Sinha is a partner in McCarthy Tétrault’s National Litigation and Dispute Resolution Group and Co-Lead of the firm’s International Arbitration Group. Her practice is focused on complex commercial and investment arbitrations and litigation, as well as cross-border and jurisdictional disputes, and geopolitical risk advisory.
Vasuda’s extensive experience and appearances as counsel before various international arbitral tribunals and courts, including SCC, ICC, UNCITRAL and ICSID tribunals and the Supreme Court of Canada, is particularly sought-after by clients with global operations and investments during periods of geopolitical and market volatility. She has represented clients on a broad variety of matters including those related to insolvency and investment treaty violations involving a variety of industries and jurisdictions.
Prior to joining the firm, Vasuda practiced for nearly a decade with a leading international law firm in Paris, France and spent a year in various chambers in London as a Fox Scholar. She also previously practised for five years with an international firm in its Toronto office, giving her a unique sense of the opportunities and challenges encountered by clients in Canada and abroad.
Active within the legal industry, Vasuda is a frequent presenter and author on timely legal topics such as damages in investment arbitration, human rights, and disputes in emerging technologies. She is also a strong supporter of diversity in the profession and has been a formal and informal mentor to young lawyers at the start of their career.
Vasuda is a member of the International Chamber of Commerce (Canada) and  Young Canadian Arbitration Practitioners, and is called to the bars in Ontario and Paris, France. She speaks English, Hindi and French.
Representative arbitration experience:
- The owners of a power generation business in relation to the termination of a development contract with a local services provider (ICC)
- Shareholders in investment treaty claims arising from a host state’s treatment and expropriation of businesses in the textile industry (ICSID)
- A Cayman entity in a breach of contract claim regarding option rights in in a shareholder agreement (ICC)
- An international energy conglomerate in investment treaty claims arising from a host state’s treatment and expropriation of its local investments, including its natural gas import business (ICSID)
- A mining company in respect of an investment treaty claim arising from a host state’s treatment and expropriation of a gold mine
- Shareholders of a gas distribution business in parallel investment treaty claims arising from a host state’s treatment of its concession rights (ICSID, UNCITRAL)
- A network of inter-related companies against a post-Soviet state in respect of various contractual breaches and investment treaty violations (ICSID, SCC)
- A global leader in the quick-service restaurant industry in a post-acquisition dispute with a former franchisee in Turkey (ICC)
Representative publications:
- Reimagining the International Legal Order, Vesselin Popovski, Ankit Malhotra Eds., (Routledge 2024) 'Pursuit of Domestic Remedies for Claims in International Law'
- Kluwer Arbitration Blog (February 2023) 'Peace River Hydro Partners v Petrowest: Are Insolvency and Arbitration at a Crossroads in the Canadian Context?'
- Romanian Arbitration Journal, Number 1/2021 (April 2021) Co-author (with Gabriel Fusea), 'Counterclaims in Investment Arbitration: Key Threshold Issues for Claimants, Respondents and Tribunals'
- Commercial Litigation and Arbitration Review, Volume 8, Number 4 (November 2019) Co-author (with Gabriel Fusea), ‘Churchill Falls (Labrador) Corp v Hydro-Québec: A Lesson in Comparative Contract Law’
- Contemporary and Emerging Issues on the Law of Damages and Valuation in International Investment Arbitration, Christina L. Beharry, Ed., Nijhoff International Investment Law Series, Volume: 11, (Boston : Brill Nijhoff, 2018) Co-author (with Noah Rubins and Baxter Roberts), ‘Approaches to Valuation in Investment Treaty Arbitration’
Representative conference and speaking engagements:
- ICC Canada Arbitration Committee Annual Conference (2024): Arbitration Feud Game Show on Investor-State Arbitrations
- Freshfields/Indo-French Chamber of Commerce/Trilegal Roundtable on disputes and compliance regimes in India and France (2024)
- 67th Union Internationale des Avocats Congress (2023): Panel on Arbitration and insolvency
- ICC Canada Arbitration Committee Annual Conference (2023): Debate on AI in arbitration
- ICCA-VanIAC Conference on Energy Infrastructure Disputes and Arbitration (2023): “Public interests in energy infrastructure disputes: Who speaks for you, me and the trees?” (Moderator)
- CanArbWeek (2023): TCAS Panel on Arbitrator Disclosure
- Centre for International Legal Studies, 11th Biennial Arbitration Symposium (2022): “How Lawyers Should Present Damages”
- TCAS Annual Conference (2022): Panel on Diversity Allyship in Arbitration
- International Centre For Alternative Dispute Resolution (India), Live Session on ADR (2020), “Alternative Dispute Resolution : International Notability”
- Canadian Institute for Advanced Legal Studies Cambridge Lectures (2019): “Electoral Interference - A Regulator's Perspective” (Moderator)
- Centre for International Legal Studies, 10th Biennial Arbitration Symposium, 2018: “Competition and Arbitration”
